subject: Mathematics (NCERT - Ganita Prakash)
chapter: Another Peek Beyond the Point (Decimals - Multiplication and Division)

activities_projects.title: Reciprocal Decimal Discovery
activities_projects.objective: Explore the relationship between fractions and terminating/recurring decimals
steps: Divide 1 by 7 using long division up to 8 decimal places
Identify the repeating block of digits in the quotient
Predict the decimal value of $2 \div 7$ by multiplying your first answer by 2
Verify your prediction with actual division
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands patterns in non-terminating recurring decimals
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of division and pattern prediction (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Infinite Zoom (Unit Conversion)
activities_projects.objective: Convert small units through multiple decimal steps
steps: Measure the thickness of a stack of 10 paper sheets in mm (e.g., 0.8 mm)
Calculate the thickness of a single sheet in mm ($0.8 \div 10$)
Convert this thickness into cm ($mm \div 10$) and then meters ($cm \div 100$)
Express the final answer in scientific notation if possible
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Masters multi-step decimal division for unit conversions
activities_projects.evaluation: Precision in decimal placement across conversions (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Algebraic Decimals
steps: Show the subtraction and division steps clearly
Create a word problem where $0.5x = 12.75$ and solve it
Explain how to clear decimals by multiplying the whole equation by 100
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct solving of the linear equation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Pendulum Period Average
activities_projects.objective: Calculate mean and error in decimal measurements
steps: Time 10 swings of a pendulum and divide by 10 to get the 'period' (e.g., 1.24s)
Repeat this 5 times to get 5 decimal values
Calculate the mean period to 3 decimal places
Determine the difference between your highest and lowest measurement
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies high-precision decimal division to physics experiments
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of the mean to specified decimal places (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Complex Area: The L-Shaped Floor
activities_projects.objective: Calculate area of composite shapes with decimal dimensions
steps: Draw an L-shaped figure made of two rectangles with sides like 2.3m, 4.5m, 1.2m, and 2.1m
Split the figure into two parts and calculate each area using $L 	imes W$
Sum the areas and find the total cost of flooring if 1 $m^2$ costs ₹450.75
Round the final cost to the nearest rupee
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Solves multi-step real-world geometry problems using decimals
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct decomposition and total cost calculation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Chemical Concentration Task
steps: If a solution has 0.045 kg of salt in 1.5 liters of water, find the salt per liter
Calculate how much salt is needed for 0.25 liters of the same solution
Express the concentration as a percentage ($salt \div water 	imes 100$)
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Uses decimals to calculate ratios and concentrations
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of the 'per liter' unit rate (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Decaying Decimal Pattern
steps: Start with the number 100. Multiply it by 0.5 repeatedly 6 times
List the sequence: 100, 50, 25...
Identify at which step the number becomes less than 1
Discuss why the product never actually reaches zero
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the behavior of repeated multiplication by decimals $< 1$
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct identification of the sequence terms (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Fuel Efficiency & Speed
steps: A vehicle covers 43.2 km in 2.4 liters of petrol
Calculate the distance covered in 1 liter
If the car travels at 40.5 km/hr, calculate the time taken to empty a full 15.5-liter tank
Show all division steps involving decimal divisors
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Masters multi-variable problems involving decimal rates
activities_projects.evaluation: Logical multi-step computation and accuracy (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Gold Smith's Precision
activities_projects.objective: Add, subtract, and multiply high-precision decimals
steps: A goldsmith has 15.450g of gold. He uses 3.125g for a ring and 5.05g for a chain
Calculate the remaining gold
If he buys 2.5 times the remaining gold, how much does he have now?
Calculate the total value if gold is ₹6250.50 per gram
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Handles high-value decimal precision in financial contexts
activities_projects.evaluation: Zero-error calculation in the multi-step process (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Decimal Divider Challenge
activities_projects.objective: Verify the relationship between dividend, divisor, and quotient
steps: Pick two decimals: $1.44$ and $1.2$. Divide $1.44 \div 1.2$
Now divide $1.2 \div 1.44$ up to 4 decimal places
Multiply the two results together
Explain why the product of $(a/b) 	imes (b/a)$ is always 1, even with decimals
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Explores the property of reciprocals in the decimal system
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct division and verification of the product (10 marks)
